title: Market Brief — Sunday, April 19, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 19% (117/333), H200 at a severe 16% (83/367), and B200 is the loosest at 24% (148/424) but still tight. B200 posted the biggest daily move at +6.2%, with H200 close behind at +5.6%, while H100 dropped 6.6% as workloads migrate to newer silicon.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 19% (117/333), H200 at a severe 16% (83/367), and B200 is the loosest at 24% (148/424) but still tight. B200 posted the biggest daily move at +6.2%, with H200 close behind at +5.6%, while H100 dropped 6.6% as workloads migrate to newer silicon.

B200 is the only chip where medium-tier inference nearly breaks even; H100 and H200 operators lose money at every tier below premium pricing. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.42/M output undercuts TCO-adjusted B200 cost of $3.79/M on Llama 70B, meaning DeepSeek is burning cash at roughly $3.37 per million tokens. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

H200 at 16% availability and 84% utilization with 52.7% annualized volatility is overvalued and due for a squeeze or a correction. B200 at $4.15 spot with 25.6pp utilization surge and the best margin profile is the undervalued compute play to accumulate now before availability tightens further.
bullets: 16% H200 availability is the tightest in the stack, qualifying as severe shortage at just 83 free units.
24% B200 availability masks a 25.6 percentage point utilization surge in 30 days, signaling rapid tightening.
$3.03 per million tokens makes B200 the cheapest inference cost, 44% below H100.
88.3% annualized B200 price volatility is the highest across all GPU classes.
$0.42 DeepSeek V3.2 output pricing implies a per-token loss of approximately $3.37/M versus TCO-adjusted B200 cost.
21.43 LLMflation index means API prices have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
